Does not form pyrochlore structure — La2Ge2O7 adopts a triclinic structure (space group P1) containing mixed Ge environments (trigermanate chains [Ge3O10] and isolated [GeO4] tetrahedra); cannot disorder via A/B antisite swapping like a pyrochlore; refs: 10.1016/j.ceramint.2020.02.133 and 10.1016/j.jlumin.2022.119295

No papers found; Ca2+ is far too large for octahedral B-site coordination and almost exclusively occupies A-sites in perovskites; charge balance (La3+×2, Ca2+, Ce4+) works on paper but structural instability prevents formation; no synthesis attempts found in literature

No papers found; Ce4+ on the B-site is problematic — large ionic radius (~0.87 Å) creates severe tolerance factor mismatch with Zn2+; experimental precedent (ACS Omega 2022, 10.1021/acsomega.2c00669) shows Ce4+ B-site double perovskites require extreme conditions even with Ba2+ A-site; Ce in La-based oxides strongly prefers A-site substitution as Ce3+

No papers found; Zn2+ strongly prefers tetrahedral coordination (incompatible with octahedral B-site in double perovskite); ZnGeO3 itself only adopts perovskite structure at >30 GPa; La2MgGeO6 is the only confirmed ambient-pressure Ge double perovskite (Mg2+ is more octahedrally stable than Zn2+)

Does not form pyrochlore structure — La2Ce2O7 adopts a disordered defect-fluorite structure (cubic, Fm-3m) with randomly distributed anion vacancies; pyrochlore superstructure peaks are absent or unresolvable; A/B site antisite disorder is not applicable as the cation sublattice is already disordered; refs: 10.3390/ma15114007 (solid-state synthesis at 1300–1400°C/6h in air confirmed) and 10.1103/PhysRevB.85.132101 (neutron/synchrotron confirmation of fluorite, not pyrochlore)
